other_observers: none--Bill Schmoker, Dave Waltman, Andy Boyce arrived minutes after the bird had flown.; Scott Sievers posted a report on CObirds that three people had reported the bird to him. The bird was coming to a feeder at Galen Koch's house. Galen Koch provided the attached photos.  On 5/4/05 Peter Gent, David Waltman, Randy Sieb
specific_location: S. 45th St, two houses north of Hanover St on the east side
description: This bird was slightly smaller and more slender than American robin with a proportionally longer tail. It's head, breast, back, end of tail and primaries were black. The belly, flanks, undertail coverts, and lesser coverts were bright lemon yellow. I could not see the rump or base of tail. The bird had two bright white wing bars, the primaries appeared to be edged in white. The bill was moderately long, slender and pointed. I didn't note the leg color.Behaviors: I observed the bird just briefly in the top of a large willow tree that had just begun to leaf out. He flew off shortly after landing.Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic
